result:
Done. The pre-registration is written to `runs/pending_experiment.json` and passes `figcheck` (the anchor and readability contract). I made no other changes — the `experiments.sqlite` modification flag comes from SQLite checkpointing on read; my queries were SELECT-only.

## What I registered for this iteration

**Experiment: Off-site distractor patching** (category: augmentation) — the first input-composition augmentation in the project's 18-experiment history.

**Why this experiment.** The history says the binding constraint is no longer lighting but a structural generalization gap: the probe behind exp 18 measured train-split crops localizing to 267–400 m median while eval-split crops sit at 708–1153 m, in every bucket. The frozen split geometry explains the failure shape precisely: every eval crop's center sits on never-trained terrain, while ~76% of eval crops still have familiar terrain entering from an edge. The kept head is a whole-crop template matcher that has only ever been trained on 100%-familiar crops — partial familiarity is literally outside its training distribution. Exp 18 attacked this gap architecturally (per-patch voting) and was just reverted, so per the plateau guidance I'm attacking the same bottleneck through a different design family rather than iterating the refuted mechanism.

**The one change** (confined to `model/train.py`'s `load_training_tensors`): with probability 0.5, a training crop gets a square block (48–96 px, 14–56% of its pixels, uniform position) overwritten with the same-position block from a different training location of the same lighting bucket — while the label and Gaussian-CE target stay at the base crop's true center. Fitting patched crops requires scoring map cells from the content consistent with one location and down-weighting the rest, which is exactly what an eval crop demands. Model, losses, decode, confidence calibration, CLI, and ONNX export stay byte-identical, so the downside is bounded near the current best (1054.87 m).

**Pre-registered prediction:** broad improvement across all 24 cells to 900–1020 m worst-case if the mechanism works (the gap is lighting-independent), with the eval-vs-train probe ratio narrowing and bright-bucket coverage rising; flat ±3% if a global-template head can't learn selective reading from augmentation alone. Deployment gates unchanged (3.3 MB / ~2.7 ms).

The implementation brief spells out the exact loop restructuring (clean donor snapshot, deterministic use of the existing seeded RNG, labels untouched) plus the fixed CLI/ONNX contracts. The harness can now run the implementation, training, and scoring stages.

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT id, metrics_json FROM experiments WHERE id=17;" | head -c 3000; echo; ls /FROZEN 2>/dev/null; cat /workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/FROZEN 2>/dev/null
tool_input.description: Get exp 17 metrics breakdown and frozen file list

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=17;" > /tmp/exp17_svg.txt; wc -c /tmp/exp17_svg.txt
tool_input.description: Dump exp 17 architecture SVG to file

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=17;" > /tmp/exp17_svg.txt && wc -c /tmp/exp17_svg.txt
tool_input.description: Dump exp 17 architecture SVG to a temp file
Check whether target files already exist

tool_input.command:
.venv/bin/python - <<'PYEOF'
"""Generate runs/pending_experiment.json for this iteration:
off-site distractor patching augmentation. SVG follows the shared visual
language of archive/arch_svg_reference.py with the mandated camera-frame
glyph and anchor contract (frozen-input x=26, frozen-output x=828)."""
import json
from pathlib import Path

INK, MUT, FAINT, ACC, OCH = "#111111", "#6b6a60", "#9b998c", "#8c2f1f", "#8a6a1e"
FONT = "Palatino,Georgia,serif"
IC = 150


def txt(x, y, s, size=10, color=MUT, w=400, anchor="middle", style="", tid=None):
    tid_attr = f"id='{tid}' " if tid else ""
    return (f"<text {tid_attr}x='{x:.0f}' y='{y:.0f}' font-family='{FONT}' font-size='{size}' "
            f"fill='{color}' font-weight='{w}' text-anchor='{anchor}' {style}>{s}</text>")


def line(x1, y1, x2, y2, color, sw=1, op=None, dash=None):
    o = f" opacity='{op}'" if op else ""
    d = f" stroke-dasharray='{dash}'" if dash else ""
    return f"<line x1='{x1:.1f}' y1='{y1:.1f}' x2='{x2:.1f}' y2='{y2:.1f}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='{sw}'{o}{d}/>"


b = []

# ---- lane labels
b.append(txt(8, 26, "INFERENCE PATH — WHAT FLIES", 9, FAINT, 600, "start", "letter-spacing='1.8'"))
b.append(txt(8, 340, "TRAINING SIGNALS — NEVER FLY", 9, OCH, 600, "start", "letter-spacing='1.8'"))

# ---- mandated camera-frame glyph, Y = 112
Y = 112
b.append(f"""<g><rect id='frozen-input' x='26' y='{Y}' width='76' height='76' fill='#f6f4ea' stroke='#9b998c' stroke-width='1.6'/><path d='M26 {Y+50} L102 {Y+24}' stroke='#e6e3d4' stroke-width='6' fill='none'/><path d='M62 {Y+2} L48 {Y+74}' stroke='#e6e3d4' stroke-width='4' fill='none'/><rect x='33' y='{Y+8}' width='13' height='9' fill='#d9d5c3' transform='rotate(-8 39 {Y+12})'/><rect x='79' y='{Y+7}' width='10' height='12' fill='#cfccbd'/><rect x='34' y='{Y+58}' width='14' height='9' fill='#d9d5c3'/><rect x='76' y='{Y+50}' width='11' height='10' fill='#cfccbd' transform='rotate(6 81 {Y+55})'/><rect x='55' y='{Y+32}' width='9' height='8' fill='#d9d5c3' opacity='.85'/><ellipse cx='88' cy='{Y+66}' rx='10' ry='7' fill='#8a6a1e' opacity='.12'/><circle cx='40' cy='{Y+28}' r='.7' fill='#6b6a60' opacity='.5'/><circle cx='70' cy='{Y+14}' r='.7' fill='#6b6a60' opacity='.5'/><circle cx='92' cy='{Y+34}' r='.7' fill='#6b6a60' opacity='.5'/><circle cx='52' cy='{Y+50}' r='.7' fill='#6b6a60' opacity='.5'/><circle cx='83' cy='{Y+70}' r='.7' fill='#6b6a60' opacity='.5'/><circle cx='31' cy='{Y+44}' r='.7' fill='#6b6a60' opacity='.5'/></g>""")
b.append(txt(64, 104, "128²×3 · ~1 m/px", 9, FAINT, style="font-style='italic'"))
b.append(txt(64, 204, "camera frame", 10.5, MUT, 600))
b.append(txt(64, 216, "one of six lighting renders", 9.5, FAINT))
b.append(txt(64, 229, "frozen contract", 8.5, FAINT, style="font-style='italic'"))

# ---- rotation fan: four 26px squares at x=145
ROT_CY = [84, 128, 172, 216]
for cy in ROT_CY:
    b.append(line(104, IC, 143, cy, FAINT, 0.8, 0.7))
for k, cy in enumerate(ROT_CY):
    x0, y0 = 145, cy - 13
    b.append(f"<rect x='{x0}' y='{y0}' width='26' height='26' fill='none' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.1'/>")
    for i in (6.5, 13, 19.5):
        b.append(line(x0 + i, y0, x0 + i, y0 + 26, INK, 0.4, 0.3))
        b.append(line(x0, y0 + i, x0 + 26, y0 + i, INK, 0.4, 0.3))
    off = [(0, 0), (19.5, 0), (19.5, 19.5), (0, 19.5)][k]
    b.append(f"<rect x='{x0 + off[0]}' y='{y0 + off[1]}' width='6.5' height='6.5' fill='{INK}' fill-opacity='0.4'/>")
b.append(txt(180, 74, "shared weights ×4", 8.5, FAINT, anchor="start", style="font-style='italic'"))
b.append(txt(158, 248, "rotation fan", 10.5, INK, 600))
b.append(txt(158, 260, "0°·90°·180°·270° — exact pixel turns", 9.5, MUT))
for cy in ROT_CY:
    b.append(line(173, cy, 228, IC, FAINT, 0.8, 0.7))

# ---- trunk: three shrinking slabs with kernel projections
def slab(x, s, d):
    y = IC - s / 2
    dx, dy = d, d * 0.55
    return (f"<polygon points='{x},{y} {x+dx},{y-dy} {x+dx+s},{y-dy} {x+s},{y}' fill='#00000010' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1'/>"
            f"<polygon points='{x+s},{y} {x+dx+s},{y-dy} {x+dx+s},{y-dy+s} {x+s},{y+s}' fill='#0000001c' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1'/>"
            f"<rect x='{x}' y='{y}' width='{s}' height='{s}' fill='#00000006' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.2'/>")

b.append(slab(230, 44, 8))
b.append(slab(300, 32, 12))
b.append(slab(360, 20, 18))

def kproj(kx, ky, ks, cx, cy, cs, color):
    out = [f"<rect x='{kx}' y='{ky}' width='{ks}' height='{ks}' fill='none' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='0.9'/>",
           f"<rect x='{cx}' y='{cy}' width='{cs}' height='{cs}' fill='{color}' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='none'/>"]
    for (px, py) in ((kx, ky), (kx + ks, ky), (kx, ky + ks), (kx + ks, ky + ks)):
        out.append(line(px, py, cx + cs / 2, cy + cs / 2, color, 0.55, 0.45))
    return "".join(out)

b.append(kproj(157, 74, 8, 237, 136, 6, FAINT))
b.append(kproj(252, 134, 11, 305, 141, 5, MUT))
b.append(kproj(316, 140, 9, 364, 145, 4, MUT))
b.append(txt(306, 210, "MobileNetV3-Small trunk", 10.5, INK, 600))
b.append(txt(306, 222, "ImageNet init (BSD-3) · gently fine-tuned", 9.5, MUT))
b.append(txt(380, 122, "8²×48 ×4", 9, FAINT, style="font-style='italic'"))

# ---- gated head as mini-fans from last slab to four per-turn field maps
FLD_CY = [84, 128, 172, 216]
for cy in FLD_CY:
    for sy in (144, 150, 156):
        for ty in (cy - 8, cy, cy + 8):
            b.append(line(400, sy, 466, ty, FAINT, 0.5, 0.4))
for k, cy in enumerate(FLD_CY):
    x0, y0 = 470, cy - 13
    b.append(f"<rect x='{x0}' y='{y0}' width='26' height='26' fill='none' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.0'/>")
    for i in range(1, 6):
        p = i * 26 / 6
        b.append(line(x0 + p, y0, x0 + p, y0 + 26, INK, 0.45, 0.5))
        b.append(line(x0, y0 + p, x0 + 26, y0 + p, INK, 0.45, 0.5))
    off = [(17.3, 4.3), (17.3, 17.3), (4.3, 17.3), (4.3, 4.3)][k]
    b.append(f"<rect x='{x0 + off[0]:.1f}' y='{y0 + off[1]:.1f}' width='4.3' height='4.3' fill='{INK}' fill-opacity='0.55'/>")
b.append(txt(483, 60, "1024 logits ×4", 9, FAINT, style="font-style='italic'"))
b.append(txt(483, 248, "four field maps", 10.5, INK, 600))
b.append(txt(483, 260, "same gated two-expert head reads each turn", 9.5, MUT))
for cy in FLD_CY:
    b.append(line(498, cy, 558, IC, FAINT, 0.8, 0.7))

# ---- voted probability field: 80px grid, 8 divisions, hot cells
GX, GS, GN = 560, 80, 8
gy0 = IC - GS / 2
b.append(f"<rect x='{GX}' y='{gy0}' width='{GS}' height='{GS}' fill='none' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.2'/>")
for i in range(1, GN):
    p = i * GS / GN
    b.append(line(GX + p, gy0, GX + p, gy0 + GS, INK, 0.45, 0.5))
    b.append(line(GX, gy0 + p, GX + GS, gy0 + p, INK, 0.45, 0.5))
for (gx, gy, o) in ((5, 2, .55), (4, 2, .28), (5, 1, .28), (6, 2, .18), (5, 3, .18), (2, 5, .12), (1, 6, .1)):
    b.append(f"<rect x='{GX + gx * 10}' y='{gy0 + gy * 10}' width='10' height='10' fill='{INK}' fill-opacity='{o}'/>")
b.append(txt(600, 102, "32×32 cells", 9, FAINT))
b.append(txt(600, 208, "voted probability field", 10.5, INK, 600))
b.append(txt(600, 220, "mean of four logit maps = geometric-mean vote", 9.5, MUT))

# ---- decode: converge lines from cell centers to crosshair at (812, IC)
TX = 812
for i in range(GN):
    for j in range(GN):
        if (i + j) % 2:
            continue
        b.append(line(GX + (j + 0.5) * 10, gy0 + (i + 0.5) * 10, TX, IC, INK, 0.55, 0.4))
b.append(f"<line x1='{TX-7}' y1='{IC}' x2='{TX+7}' y2='{IC}' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
         f"<line x1='{TX}' y1='{IC-7}' x2='{TX}' y2='{IC+7}' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
         f"<circle cx='{TX}' cy='{IC}' r='3.9' fill='none' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.4'/>"
         f"<circle cx='{TX}' cy='{IC}' r='1.5' fill='{INK}'/>")
b.append(txt(712, 72, "β-sharpened soft-argmax", 10.5, INK, 600))
b.append(txt(712, 84, "commits to the hottest mode (unchanged)", 9.5, MUT))

# ---- frozen output block
b.append(txt(828, 132, "frozen contract", 8.5, FAINT, anchor="start", style="font-style='italic'"))
b.append(txt(828, 148, "(lat, lon, confidence)", 13, MUT, 600, "start", tid="frozen-output"))
b.append(txt(828, 162, "position fix + confidence", 9, FAINT, anchor="start"))

# ---- confidence branch
b.append(line(740, 185, 740, 254, FAINT, 1, dash="2 4"))
b.append(f"<path d='M 726,268 A 14 14 0 0 1 754,268' fill='none' stroke='{MUT}' stroke-width='1.2'/>")
b.append(line(740, 268, 746.3, 259.0, MUT, 1.4))
b.append(f"<circle cx='740' cy='268' r='1.6' fill='{MUT}'/>")
b.append(txt(740, 286, "confidence 0–1", 9, MUT))
b.append(txt(740, 298, "reads the voted map’s shape (unchanged)", 8.5, FAINT))
b.append(f"<path d='M 758,268 H 858 V 182' fill='none' stroke='{FAINT}' stroke-width='0.8'/>")
b.append(f"<path d='M 858,176 l -3,6 h 6 Z' fill='{FAINT}'/>")
b.append(txt(800, 258, "may abstain", 8.5, FAINT, style="font-style='italic'"))

# ---- ochre unchanged-losses note
b.append(line(620, 226, 620, 368, OCH, 1, dash="2 4"))
b.append(txt(632, 372, "position + confidence losses — all unchanged", 10, OCH, 600, "start"))
b.append(txt(632, 384, "Gaussian-CE + decode L2 + conf BCE — still graded at the true centre", 9.5, OCH, anchor="start"))

# ---- THE CHANGE (red): off-site distractor patching, training-only
b.append(f"<path d='M 238,376 V 312 H 64 V 242' fill='none' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 4' opacity='0.9'/>")
b.append(f"<path d='M 64,236 l -3,6 h 6 Z' fill='{ACC}'/>")
b.append(txt(254, 306, "half of each bucket’s training crops are patched before batching", 8.5, ACC, anchor="start", style="font-style='italic'"))

def terrain_sq(x0, y0):
    out = [f"<rect x='{x0}' y='{y0}' width='56' height='56' fill='{OCH}' fill-opacity='0.05' stroke='{OCH}' stroke-width='1.2'/>"]
    for i in (14, 28, 42):
        out.append(line(x0 + i, y0, x0 + i, y0 + 56, OCH, 0.4, 0.25))
        out.append(line(x0, y0 + i, x0 + 56, y0 + i, OCH, 0.4, 0.25))
    return "".join(out)

b.append(terrain_sq(70, 380))
b.append(f"<rect x='88' y='396' width='26' height='26' fill='{ACC}' fill-opacity='0.08' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1.1'/>")
b.append(txt(98, 452, "crop from elsewhere", 10.5, OCH, 600))
b.append(txt(98, 464, "same lighting bucket", 9.5, OCH))
b.append(line(116, 409, 200, 409, ACC, 1))
b.append(f"<path d='M 206,409 l -6,-3 v 6 Z' fill='{ACC}'/>")
b.append(txt(170, 398, "lift a 48–96 px block", 8.5, ACC, style="font-style='italic'"))
b.append(terrain_sq(210, 380))
b.append(f"<rect x='226' y='396' width='26' height='26' fill='{ACC}' fill-opacity='0.10' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1.2'/>")
b.append(line(233, 408, 243, 408, OCH, 1.3))
b.append(line(238, 403, 238, 413, OCH, 1.3))
b.append(f"<circle cx='238' cy='408' r='2.75' fill='none' stroke='{OCH}' stroke-width='1.3'/>")
b.append(txt(238, 452, "patched training crop", 10.5, ACC, 600))
b.append(txt(238, 464, "label: still the true centre", 9.5, ACC))
b.append(txt(400, 424, "off-site distractor patching — p = 0.5, training only", 10.5, ACC, 600, "start"))
for i, s in enumerate([
    "eval crops sit on never-trained blocks: the centre terrain is always unfamiliar, familiar terrain",
    "pokes in from an edge (~76% of eval crops contain some trained pixels). The probe behind exp 18:",
    "train-split crops localize to 267–400 m, eval-split to 708–1153 m — a ~3× structural gap, in every bucket.",
    "Training crops were always 100% familiar, so the whole-crop head never practised partial recognition;",
    "patched crops force it to answer from the content consistent with one place and down-weight the rest.",
]):
    b.append(txt(400, 438 + 12 * i, s, 9.5, ACC, anchor="start"))

SVG = f"<svg viewBox='0 0 980 512' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'>{''.join(b)}</svg>"

exp = {
    "title": "Off-site distractor patching: half the training crops carry a pasted block of terrain from elsewhere, labels unchanged",
    "category": "augmentation",
    "hypothesis": (
        "The binding constraint is the held-out-location generalization gap the exp-18 probe measured directly: "
        "train-split crops localize to 267-400 m median while eval-split crops sit at 708-1153 m -- a ~3x structural gap, "
        "present in every lighting bucket. The frozen split's geometry says exactly what an eval crop looks like: its "
        "center pixel always lies in a 360-px eval block whose pixels were never trained on (train windows are dropped "
        "if they touch an eval block), while ~76% of eval crops still contain SOME train-seen terrain entering from an "
        "edge or corner of the 182-px window. So the canonical eval crop is unfamiliar terrain around the center with "
        "familiar terrain at the periphery. The kept head is a whole-crop template matcher (GAP + 512-d layout code -> "
        "FC 1024), and every single crop it has ever been trained on was 100% familiar terrain -- partial familiarity is "
        "literally outside its training distribution, so at eval the unfamiliar portion shifts the descriptor away from "
        "every learned template: fields go diffuse (night coverage sits at the abstention floor) or commit to a lookalike. "
        "Exp 18 attacked this same gap architecturally (64 per-patch field votes) and was reverted (1135.85 m) -- forcing "
        "each 16-px patch to localize alone discarded the global-arrangement signal that carries most of the "
        "discrimination. The materially new angle: keep the kept architecture untouched and make partial familiarity the "
        "TRAINING condition instead. With probability 0.5 a training crop gets a square block (side 48-96 px, i.e. "
        "14-56% of its pixels, uniform position) overwritten with the same-position block from a different training "
        "location of the same lighting bucket, while the label and Gaussian-CE target stay at the base crop's true "
        "center. The pasted content is a worst-case stand-in for eval's unfamiliar content -- not merely uninformative "
        "but actively misleading, since its texture genuinely belongs elsewhere on the map. The only way to fit patched "
        "crops is to score map cells from the subset of content consistent with a single location and down-weight "
        "content that disagrees -- precisely the computation an eval crop demands and that nothing in the current "
        "training distribution has ever required. The confidence head trains on the same crops, so it simultaneously "
        "learns to flag conflicted content -- the exact crops it should abstain on at eval. This is the first "
        "input-composition augmentation in the project's history (exp 6, the only prior augmentation experiment, "
        "resampled rotations; it never altered crop content)."
    ),
    "method": (
        "One change, entirely inside model/train.py's load_training_tensors: collect each bucket's extracted crops "
        "into per-bucket lists; after the three-realization extraction loop, for each crop independently with p=0.5 "
        "paste a square block (side ~ uniform int in [48,96] px, top-left uniform over valid positions) copied from "
        "the SAME position in a different, pre-patching crop of the same bucket (donors read from a clean snapshot). "
        "Labels, Gaussian-CE targets, loss_fn, model.py, decode, confidence calibration, CLI, and ONNX export are all "
        "untouched."
    ),
    "expected_outcome": (
        "The gap is lighting-independent, so a real effect must be broad: predicted primary worst-case median 900-1020 m "
        "(from 1054.87 m) with all 24 area x bucket cells moving together rather than one cell fixed. Mechanism-specific "
        "signatures to check: (a) the eval-vs-train probe ratio narrows (baseline berlin midday 1153 vs 400 m, prignitz "
        "708 vs 267 m); (b) coverage rises modestly in bright buckets (fields stay committed on partially-familiar "
        "crops instead of going diffuse); (c) the conf head's calibration keep-rates stay >= 0.40 per bucket since "
        "calibration crops are clean. Null: a global-template head cannot be taught selective reading by input "
        "augmentation alone -> metric flat within +/-3%. Downside bounded near current best: half the training crops "
        "remain exactly exp 17's distribution and the model/loss/decode/calibration are byte-identical, so the "
        "uniform-field -> map-center floor is unchanged. Deployment gates identical (3302661 bytes, ~2.7 ms host "
        "latency proxy); training wall time +<1 min/area (numpy block copies)."
    ),
    "init_strategy": "pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0..8] (torchvision, BSD-3) — unchanged from exp 11",
    "eli5": (
        "When the drone flies over ground the model never studied, most of the picture is new to it -- but a corner "
        "usually shows land it does know. Until now, every practice picture the model ever saw was 100% familiar "
        "ground, so it never learned what to do with a half-strange view: one odd patch throws off its whole answer. "
        "We now sabotage half of the practice pictures by pasting in a square from somewhere else in the same city, "
        "while keeping the answer key unchanged. To keep scoring well, the model must learn to base its answer on the "
        "parts of the picture that fit together and shrug off the parts that don't -- like solving a jigsaw with a few "
        "pieces from the wrong box mixed in."
    ),
    "architecture": {"stages": [
        {"name": "Camera frame", "detail": "128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Rotation fan", "detail": "the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Feature extractor", "detail": "ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Layout summary", "detail": "a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Lighting gate", "detail": "a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Probability map", "detail": "two scorers read each of the four turned copies and the gate blends their per-cell guesses; the four resulting heat maps are averaged into one voted map — a location only stays hot if it wins from every direction", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Decode", "detail": "the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Confidence", "detail": "the model inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Output", "detail": "(lat, lon, confidence)", "changed": False},
        {"name": "Training signal", "detail": "unchanged — same position and confidence grading as before", "changed": False, "train_only": True},
        {"name": "Training data", "detail": "each practice image still comes from one of three independently rolled versions of the lighting simulator; NEW: half of them additionally get a square patch (48–96 px, 14–56% of the picture) pasted in from a different spot in the same area and lighting, while the answer key still points at the original location — the model must answer from the parts that fit together and distrust content that belongs somewhere else, which is exactly the situation it faces over held-out terrain", "changed": True, "train_only": True},
    ]},
    "architecture_svg": SVG,
    "implementation_brief": (
        "Change exactly one file: model/train.py. Do NOT touch model/model.py, pipeline/, or anything in /FROZEN.\n"
        "\n"
        "1. Module-top constants (next to TRAIN_REALIZATIONS): add\n"
        "     PATCH_P = 0.5          # fraction of training crops that receive an off-site distractor block\n"
        "     PATCH_SIDE_MIN = 48    # pasted block side, px\n"
        "     PATCH_SIDE_MAX = 96\n"
        "\n"
        "2. In load_training_tensors, restructure ONLY the per-bucket inner loop. Currently, for each bucket, crops are "
        "appended directly to the global xs/ys lists across the three realizations. Instead: (a) collect this bucket's "
        "extracted crops into local lists bucket_xs (uint8 HxWx3 arrays from extract_crop) and bucket_ys "
        "(crop_center_norm tuples), preserving the existing realization structure, picks, rng call order for angles, "
        "and image loading/deletion exactly as they are; (b) after the bucket's realization loop finishes, apply "
        "distractor patching: take a clean snapshot first, e.g. clean = list(bucket_xs), then for each index i in "
        "range(len(bucket_xs)): if rng.random() < PATCH_P: choose a donor j uniformly from the other crops "
        "(j = int(rng.integers(len(clean) - 1)); j = j + 1 if j >= i else j), draw s = int(rng.integers(PATCH_SIDE_MIN, "
        "PATCH_SIDE_MAX + 1)), h, w = bucket_xs[i].shape[:2], y0 = int(rng.integers(0, h - s + 1)), "
        "x0 = int(rng.integers(0, w - s + 1)), then patched = clean[i].copy(); "
        "patched[y0:y0+s, x0:x0+s] = clean[j][y0:y0+s, x0:x0+s]; bucket_xs[i] = patched. Donors MUST come from the "
        "clean snapshot (never from an already-patched crop) and the same-position block is copied (same y0/x0 in "
        "donor and base). Labels are NOT touched: bucket_ys stays exactly as extracted; (c) xs.extend(bucket_xs); "
        "ys.extend(bucket_ys) at the end of the bucket iteration. Net effect: identical tensor shapes and dtypes, "
        "~half the crops carry one pasted square block from another location of the same bucket, all labels/targets "
        "unchanged.\n"
        "\n"
        "3. Use the existing rng (np.random.default_rng(seed)) for all new draws so runs stay deterministic given "
        "--seed. Keep the patching loop after ALL of the bucket's realizations so donors can come from any of the "
        "three realizations (they share the lighting bucket, which is what matters for photometric consistency).\n"
        "\n"
        "4. Lineage: add to the info dict returned by train_area: "
        "\"distractor_patching\": {\"p\": PATCH_P, \"side_px\": [PATCH_SIDE_MIN, PATCH_SIDE_MAX]}.\n"
        "\n"
        "5. Everything else stays byte-identical: build_model/loss_fn/export_onnx usage, the epoch loop, optimizer "
        "param groups and LRs, calibrate_conf_shift (calibration crops remain clean/unpatched — intentional), "
        "TRAIN_REALIZATIONS=3 logic, uint8 storage with per-minibatch float conversion.\n"
        "\n"
        "Fixed contracts to preserve (the frozen scorer depends on them): train.py CLI stays exactly "
        "`python -m model.train --area A --out-dir D [--data-dir P] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket M] "
        "[--seed S]`, exporting one ONNX per area at <out-dir>/models/<area>.onnx that takes a 1x3x128x128 float32 "
        "input in [0,1] and returns [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1], and appending "
        "the per-area info dict to <out-dir>/train_info.json. model/model.py's docstring contract and the deployment "
        "gates (ONNX <= 4 MiB, host latency proxy <= 250 ms) are unaffected since the model is untouched."
    ),
}

out = Path("runs/pending_experiment.json")
out.write_text(json.dumps(exp, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
print(f"wrote {out} ({out.stat().st_size} bytes; svg {len(SVG)} bytes)")
PYEOF
tool_input.description: Generate runs/pending_experiment.json with design + SVG figure
